varieties.name: Yucca elephantipes (Spineless Yucca, Giant Yucca)
varieties.origin: Mexico and Guatemala, introduced to cultivation in 1800s
varieties.traits: Multi-trunked tree-like form, 3-9m height, thick elephant-foot base, soft-tipped sword-shaped leaves 60-120cm long, deep green color
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, fast-growing for a yucca, develops multiple branches over time
varieties.availability: Widely available from Australian nurseries including Benara Nurseries, Australian Plants Online, and most humid subtropical climates garden centers

varieties.name: Yucca gigantea 'Variegata' (Variegated Spineless Yucca)
varieties.origin: Cultivated selection from Y. gigantea, developed in horticultural trade
varieties.traits: Similar to species but with pale green leaves featuring broad cream-colored stripes along edges, 30-60cm leaves, 4-6m height potential
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth, slightly slower than green form due to reduced chlorophyll
varieties.availability: Available from specialty nurseries in Australia, North Carolina Extension program notes cultivar availability

varieties.name: Yucca gigantea 'Marginata' (Margined Spineless Yucca)
varieties.origin: Horticultural selection
varieties.traits: Pale green leaves with narrow cream-colored stripe on both leaf edges, small teeth on margins, soft tips, multi-stemmed growth
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth rate, forms attractive clumps
varieties.availability: Specialty plant retailers, North Carolina Extension documentation

varieties.name: Yucca aloifolia (Spanish Bayonet, Aloe Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA, Caribbean, first yucca in cultivation (1605), naturalized in Queensland and NSW
varieties.traits: Tree-like form to 3m+, stiff sword-shaped leaves 30-60cm long, very sharp spine tips, denticulate margins, dark green, densely branched
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth, produces edible fruits and flowers
varieties.availability: Commonly available in Queensland nurseries, listed as naturalized in Western Australia, Queensland, and NSW

varieties.name: Yucca aloifolia 'Variegata' (Variegated Spanish Bayonet)
varieties.origin: Horticultural selection from Y. aloifolia
varieties.traits: Stiff leaves with cream or yellow variegation, sharp-tipped, smaller and more compact than species, 1.5-2.5m height

varieties.name: Yucca gloriosa (Spanish Dagger, Mound Lily Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA (North Carolina to Florida), introduced to Britain from Carolinas pre-1800s
varieties.traits: Shrub to 2.5m, develops woody trunk with age, dark green stiff leaves 30-50cm long, sharp brown terminal spine, smooth margins, forms suckering colonies
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, slow-growing but long-lived, produces edible flowers and fruits
varieties.availability: Widely available in Queensland nurseries, Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit winner

varieties.name: Yucca gloriosa 'Variegata' (Variegated Spanish Dagger)
varieties.origin: Hybrid (Yucca aloifolia × filamentosa), shared by JC Raulston Arboretum mid-1980s
varieties.traits: Blue-green rigid leaves bordered with wide margins that emerge gold then change to cream, 48 inches height in 10 years, 2-3' wide trunked clumps
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, Plant Delights Nursery, Architectural Plants UK (ships internationally), Monrovia

varieties.name: Yucca gloriosa 'Bright Star' (Bright Star Spanish Dagger)
varieties.origin: Cultivar of Y. gloriosa
varieties.traits: Green leaves with yellow margins, develops light pinkish-red hue in temperatures below -17°C, stiff sharp leaves in rosette, 2-4 feet long leaves
varieties.availability: Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Yucca desmetiana (Soft-leaved Compact Yucca)
varieties.origin: Mexico, sometimes recorded as Yucca de Smetiana
varieties.traits: Rosette of broad leaves, green maturing to lilac and burgundy, 1.2m height × 1m width, small thorns along outer edges of foliage, compact form
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth, develops attractive coloration over time
varieties.availability: Australian Plants Online, popular when in stock, thrives in both Queensland and Victoria

varieties.name: Yucca rostrata (Beaked Yucca, Big Bend Yucca)
varieties.origin: Northern Mexico (Chihuahua, Coahuila), southwestern USA (Texas)
varieties.traits: Stunning single trunk to 4.5m, topped with perfectly symmetrical spherical pom-pom head, pale blue-green almost silvery leaves to 60cm long, narrow sword-shaped leaves that bend slightly, 2.4-5m height × 1-3m spread
varieties.yield_notes: Slow-growing, flowers on mature plants (4-5 years old)
varieties.availability: Designer Trees Australia (grown from seed on-site), Queensland nurseries specializing in architectural plants

varieties.name: Yucca filamentosa (Adam's Needle)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA (Florida to Virginia), heirloom variety cultivated since pre-1800s
varieties.traits: Clump-forming, stemless, 3 feet tall × 3 feet wide, dramatic blue-green dagger-like leaves with white thread-like filaments on margins, evergreen
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable flowering, forms expanding clumps through offsets
varieties.availability: Widely available in Australia through specialty native and exotic plant nurseries

varieties.name: Yucca filamentosa 'Color Guard' (Color Guard Adam's Needle)
varieties.origin: Japanese cultivar from Terra Nova Nurseries
varieties.traits: Sword-shaped leaves with bold central stripes of bright canary-yellow against celadon green edges, 2-3 feet tall × 2-3 feet wide, pink tones in winter, curly white margin fibers
varieties.yield_notes: Growth superior to other Y. filamentosa clones, coral winter color along stripes
varieties.availability: Monrovia, Missouri Botanical Garden, specialty nurseries, Australian imports

varieties.name: Yucca filamentosa 'Golden Sword' (Golden Sword Yucca)
varieties.origin: Horticultural selection
varieties.traits: Dramatic sword-shaped yellow leaves with dark green edge, 24-36 inches tall × 24-36 inches wide, leaf tips sometimes droop with age, blue-green blades with broad central stripe of golden yellow
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, foliage color best from fall to spring
varieties.availability: Monrovia, White Flower Farm, Great Plant Picks selection, Lowe's, widely available

varieties.name: Yucca filamentosa 'Bright Edge' (Bright Edge Variegated Yucca)
varieties.origin: Dwarf cultivar selection
varieties.traits: Compact form, golden-yellow margined sword-like foliage, blue-green center with bright yellow edges, 2-3 feet tall × 2-3 feet wide, rose tones in winter, upright growth
varieties.yield_notes: Dwarf growth habit, forms attractive clumps 3-4 feet tall
varieties.availability: Growing Wild Nursery, Loen Nursery, Devil Mountain Wholesale Nursery, Briggs Nursery, Australian specialty importers

varieties.name: Yucca filamentosa 'Excalibur' (Excalibur Adam's Needle)
varieties.origin: Bred by American Beauties Native Plants
varieties.traits: Striking blue-green to gray-green sword-shaped leaves, stiff upright form, edged with curly white filaments, compact uniform growth habit, 1.5-2 feet height × 3-4 feet spread
varieties.yield_notes: Slow-growing, reliable flowering on mature plants
varieties.availability: Bloomindesigns.com, American Beauties Native Plants, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Yucca recurvifolia (Curve-leaf Yucca, Pendulous Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA, possibly hybrid origin (Y. aloifolia × Y. flaccida)
varieties.traits: Soft-leaf variety, long flexible green leaves that curve downward in fountain-like form, forms short trunk to 6 feet (usually unbranched), graceful arching habit, 4-8 feet tall
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, develops architectural form over time

varieties.name: Yucca flaccida (Weak-leaf Yucca, Adam's Needle)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA, similar to Y. filamentosa with minor differences, historical variety
varieties.traits: Slender thinner more flexible leaves than Y. filamentosa, finer straighter filaments, gorgeous rosette form, weak/drooping leaves that fold, just over 50cm height

varieties.name: Yucca baccata (Banana Yucca, Datil Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southwestern USA and northern Mexico, traditional food source for Native Americans, historical cultivar
varieties.traits: Thick flexible leaves with curled fibers on edges, 3-10 feet height, dull green to blue-gray leaves, clump-forming to 16 feet wide, deeply concave leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Produces edible fruits, flowers once yearly or in favorable conditions
varieties.availability: Specialty drought-tolerant plant nurseries, seed available

varieties.name: Yucca brevifolia (Joshua Tree)
varieties.origin: Mojave Desert California, Nevada, Utah, Arizona - iconic desert symbol
varieties.traits: Tree-like form 15-50 feet (usually 30 feet), branching arms, spiky green leaves, classic symbol of California desert, greenish-white flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely slow-growing, can live many decades
varieties.availability: Rare in Australia, specialty desert plant collectors, seed available

varieties.name: Yucca treculeana (Don Quixote's Lace, Spanish Dagger)
varieties.origin: Texas, northeastern Mexico, common name references the plant's appearance
varieties.traits: Large tree-like yucca, impressive stature, stiff sword-like leaves, multi-branched form
varieties.availability: Specialty collectors, rare in Australian trade

varieties.name: Yucca faxoniana (Spanish Dagger, Giant Dagger)
varieties.origin: West Texas, northern Mexico (Chihuahua)
varieties.traits: Tall robust yucca with very stiff sword-like leaves, dense rosettes, can reach 20+ feet, very sharp leaf tips
varieties.availability: Rare specialty collectors, seed occasionally available

varieties.name: Yucca glauca (Soapweed Yucca, Great Plains Yucca)
varieties.origin: Great Plains USA (Texas to Montana/North Dakota), traditional soap source, historical use
varieties.traits: Compact clump-forming, narrow leaves with white margins, stemless or short-stemmed, 2-4 feet height
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable flowering, roots historically used for soap
varieties.availability: Specialty native plant nurseries, seed available

varieties.name: Yucca elata (Soaptree Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southwestern USA and northern Mexico
varieties.traits: Tall slender trunk to 15-20 feet, narrow grass-like leaves, creates distinct architectural form, often multi-headed
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth for a tree yucca
varieties.availability: Specialty desert plant nurseries, seed sources

varieties.name: Hesperoyucca whipplei (Our Lord's Candle, Chaparral Yucca)
varieties.origin: California and Baja California, formerly Yucca whipplei, reclassified to Hesperoyucca
varieties.traits: Rigid sharp leaves forming dense rosette, up to 1.3 feet height for plant, monocarpic (dies after flowering)
varieties.yield_notes: Single flowering event before death, but offsets grow in place of mother plant
varieties.availability: Specialty California native plant nurseries, seed available, possibly naturalized in Australia

varieties.name: Yucca thompsoniana (Thompson's Yucca, Beaked Yucca)
varieties.origin: Texas and northern Mexico (Coahuila, Chihuahua)
varieties.traits: Densely leaved globe of pale bluish to blue-green leaves, 3.3 feet trunk height, 8-10 feet tall overall, reliable branch-former with up to 10 branches, slightly stiffer and rougher leaves than Y. rostrata
varieties.yield_notes: Slow-growing, develops attractive multi-branched form, neat appearance (older leaves hang down nicely)
varieties.availability: Specialty architectural plant nurseries, sometimes confused with Y. rostrata in trade

varieties.name: Yucca rigida (Blue Yucca, Silver-leaf Yucca)
varieties.origin: Northern Mexico
varieties.traits: One of most beautiful stemmed yuccas, very stiff sharp pale blue to blue-green leaves, 10+ feet height, can have many heads or just 1-2, extremely sharp leaf tips
varieties.availability: Specialty architectural plant collectors, Designer Trees type nurseries

varieties.name: Yucca schidigera (Mojave Yucca)
varieties.origin: Mojave and Sonoran deserts, California, Nevada, Arizona
varieties.traits: Small tree to 16 feet, stiff dagger-like leaves, forms branching trunk, drought-adapted
varieties.availability: Specialty desert plant nurseries

varieties.name: Yucca gigantea (Spineless Yucca, Giant Yucca)
varieties.origin: Central America (Mexico, Guatemala), widely cultivated in tropics
varieties.traits: Large upright tree to 15-30 feet outdoors, thick woody trunk, sword-shaped blue-green leaves 12-18 inches long, spineless soft tips
varieties.yield_notes: Fast-growing for a yucca, readily branches
varieties.availability: Commonly available from Indian nurseries, tropical plant suppliers, tropical monsoon climates garden centers

varieties.name: Yucca elephantipes (Spineless Yucca)
varieties.origin: Mexico and Central America, tropical native
varieties.traits: Identical to Y. gigantea (same plant, different names used in literature), fast-growing tree-like form, elephant-foot trunk base, soft-tipped leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Rapid growth in tropical conditions, can reach 8-9 meters
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout India, tropical monsoon climates nurseries, common houseplant

varieties.name: Yucca aloifolia (Spanish Bayonet, Dagger Plant)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA, Mexico, Caribbean - coastal warm climate native
varieties.traits: Tree-like shrub to 10 feet, stiff dangerous leaves 12-20 inches long, sharp-tipped and denticulate margins, densely arranged leaves
varieties.availability: Available from tropical plant nurseries, Indian garden centers, naturalized in some tropical areas

varieties.name: Yucca gloriosa (Spanish Dagger, Mound Lily Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA coastal habitats, sand dunes
varieties.traits: Shrub to 16 feet, develops woody trunk, stiff leaves 30-50cm with sharp spine, eventually tree-like form
varieties.availability: Tropical plant importers, specialty nurseries in India

varieties.name: Yucca recurvifolia (Pendulous Yucca, Soft Leaf Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA, possibly hybrid (Y. aloifolia × Y. flaccida)
varieties.traits: Graceful fountain-like form, soft arching leaves that droop, 4-8 feet tall, develops short trunk, safer than sharp-leaved varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Good growth, architectural form develops over time
varieties.availability: Available through tropical plant specialists, Indian importers

varieties.name: Yucca filamentosa (Adam's Needle)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA, historical heirloom variety
varieties.traits: Clump-forming, stemless, 3 feet rosettes, blue-green leaves with white filaments
varieties.availability: Specialty importers, online plant retailers shipping to India

varieties.name: Yucca filamentosa 'Color Guard' (Variegated Adam's Needle)
varieties.origin: Japanese cultivar from Terra Nova Nurseries
varieties.traits: Yellow-centered variegated leaves, 2-3 feet clumps, pink winter tones
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, superior growth compared to other Y. filamentosa clones
varieties.availability: International plant shipments, specialty collectors in India

varieties.name: Yucca flaccida (Weak-leaf Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA, historical variety
varieties.traits: Soft flexible drooping leaves, 50cm height, delicate filaments

varieties.name: Yucca desmetiana (Soft Compact Yucca)
varieties.origin: Mexico (tropical region)
varieties.traits: Compact rosettes, burgundy coloration, 1.2m × 1m, soft-leaved
varieties.availability: Tropical plant specialists, may be available through Indian nursery networks

varieties.name: Yucca rostrata (Beaked Yucca)
varieties.origin: Northern Mexico, southwestern USA deserts
varieties.traits: Spectacular architectural form, blue-silvery leaves, 2.4-5m trunk
varieties.availability: Rare in India, specialty collectors only

varieties.name: Yucca thompsoniana (Thompson's Yucca)
varieties.origin: Texas, northern Mexico dry regions
varieties.traits: Blue-green globe of leaves, 8-10 feet with branches, neater than most yuccas
varieties.availability: Rare in Indian trade, specialty collectors

varieties.name: Yucca baccata (Banana Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southwestern USA deserts, historical Native American food source
varieties.traits: Clumping form, thick leaves with fibers, edible fruits
varieties.availability: Not recommended for tropical monsoon climates conditions

varieties.name: Yucca glauca (Soapweed Yucca)
varieties.origin: Great Plains USA, traditional soapweed
varieties.traits: Compact clumps, narrow leaves with white margins
varieties.availability: Not suitable for tropical monsoon climates climate

varieties.name: Yucca elata (Soaptree Yucca)
varieties.origin: Southwestern USA and Mexico deserts
varieties.traits: Tall tree form to 20 feet, narrow leaves
varieties.availability: Limited suitability for tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Yucca filifera (Tree Yucca, Palma China)
varieties.origin: Central Mexico
varieties.traits: Tall tree-like form, slender trunk topped with cluster of long arching leaves, can reach 20+ feet, tropical and exotic appearance
varieties.availability: Rare in India, specialty collectors, seed occasionally available

varieties.name: Yucca rigida (Blue Yucca)
varieties.origin: Northern Mexico deserts
varieties.traits: Stunning pale blue leaves, very stiff and sharp, 10+ feet
varieties.availability: Not suitable for tropical monsoon climates conditions

varieties.name: Yucca schidigera (Mojave Yucca)
varieties.origin: Mojave and Sonoran deserts
varieties.traits: Tree to 16 feet, stiff dagger leaves
varieties.availability: Not suitable for tropical monsoon climates climate

varieties.name: Yucca brevifolia (Joshua Tree)
varieties.origin: Mojave Desert, California
varieties.traits: Iconic tree form, 15-50 feet, branching arms
varieties.availability: Not suitable for tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Hesperoyucca whipplei (Our Lord's Candle)
varieties.origin: California and Baja California chaparral
varieties.traits: Rigid sharp rosette, monocarpic (dies after flowering)
varieties.yield_notes: Single flowering then death, offsets continue
varieties.availability: Not suitable for tropical monsoon climates climate

varieties.name: Yucca treculeana (Texas Bayonet)
varieties.origin: Texas and northeastern Mexico
varieties.traits: Large tree-like yucca, stiff sword leaves
varieties.availability: Limited suitability for tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Yucca faxoniana (Spanish Dagger, Giant Dagger)
varieties.origin: West Texas and northern Mexico deserts
varieties.traits: Tall robust form to 20+ feet, very stiff leaves
varieties.availability: Not suitable for tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Yucca gigantea 'Variegata' (Variegated Giant Yucca)
varieties.origin: Cultivated selection from tropical Y. gigantea
varieties.traits: Cream-striped leaves, similar form to species but variegated
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, slightly slower than green form
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, horticultural importers to India

varieties.name: Yucca aloifolia 'Variegata' (Variegated Spanish Bayonet)
varieties.origin: Cultivar of tropical/coastal Y. aloifolia
varieties.traits: Variegated leaves with cream/yellow stripes, compact form
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical plant suppliers

varieties.name: Yucca gloriosa 'Variegata' (Variegated Spanish Dagger)
varieties.origin: Hybrid cultivar (Y. aloifolia × filamentosa)
varieties.traits: Gold-cream margined blue-green leaves
varieties.availability: International plant specialists, RHS Award winner available through imports

varieties.name: Yucca gloriosa 'Bright Star' (Bright Star Spanish Dagger)
varieties.origin: Cultivar of Y. gloriosa
varieties.traits: Yellow-margined green leaves, compact form
varieties.availability: RHS Award cultivar, specialty imports
